title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Runtime Contract: Content Decoding Limit
summary: A body reader allocates from attacker Content-Length, treats premature EOF as success, consumes the next pipelined message, and reuses a connection after incomplete content. Planned artifact: Add and verify the content decoding limit increment in bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep message framing separate from content coding and bound both encoded and decoded sizes against expansion attacks.
Use close-delimited response content only in permitted response contexts and mark the connection non-reusable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Content Decoding Limit · Theory Content Decoding Limit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 6778 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Content Decoding Limit contract for the supplied data.
